deck_meta.title: Software 3.0 and the Rise of Agentic Engineering
deck_meta.subtitle: Karpathy's framework for the AI-native software era

slides.title:
Software 3.0 and the
Rise of Agentic
Engineering
slides.subtitle: Karpathy at Sequoia Ascent 2026 — the context window is the new program, and the programmer is the orchestrator.

slides.title:
From writing code —
to orchestrating agents.
slides.lede: Karpathy frames a step change in late 2025: LLM-driven agents stopped being autocomplete and started being a programmable layer. The unit of programming shifted from typing lines to delegating macro actions.

from_block.label: Old default workflow
points: Humans write explicit code (Software 1.0)
Humans curate datasets, train weights (Software 2.0)
Agents useful but require frequent correction
Programmer = primary author of every line

to_block.label: Software 3.0 default
points: Humans program LLMs through context, tools, examples
Context window becomes the main lever
Programmer delegates: implement, refactor, research, deploy
Programmer = orchestrator of fallible agents

slides.section_title: Three shifts that define the new era.
preview: December 2025 marked an agentic step change
Software 3.0: the context window is the new program
Some apps stop existing as apps

slides.title: The headline shifts.
slides.subtitle: Software is being refactored around agents and verifiable feedback loops.

stats.label: Software paradigm
stats.note: 1.0 explicit code → 2.0 learned weights → 3.0 prompts, context, tools, memory. The interpreter is the LLM.

stats.label: Agentic inflection
stats.note: Generated chunks got larger, more coherent, more reliable. The unit of programming shifted from lines to macro actions.

stats.label: Capability levers
stats.note: Capability spike ≈ verifiability × training attention × data coverage × economic value. Frontier models spike where labs trained hardest.

stats.label: Agentic engineer leverage
stats.note: Mastery of agentic workflows may extend the old “10x engineer” gap by an order of magnitude — orchestration is the new ceiling skill.

slides.footer_note: Source: §1 Agentic Inflection · §2 Software 3.0 · §6 Jagged Intelligence · §8 Hiring

slides.title:
What you can specify
vs. what you can verify.
slides.subtitle: The dividing line between classical software and the LLM-RL frontier.
slides.primary_quote: Traditional software automates what you can specify. LLMs and reinforcement learning automate what you can verify.
slides.secondary_quote: Capability spike ≈ verifiability × training attention × data coverage × economic value.
slides.implication: If your task is resettable, repeatable, and rewardable — models will fly. If not, they will fail in surprisingly basic ways. The founder question becomes: are you on the model's rails, or do you need your own evals, fine-tuning, or RL environment?

slides.section_title: Where the new opportunity actually sits.
preview: Some apps disappear into direct model transformations
The new user is the human's agent
Verifiable environments are the new wedge

slides.title:
Speeding up the old app
vs. dissolving it.
slides.subtitle: AI is not just faster scaffolding — for some workflows, the scaffolding disappears entirely.

points: OCR pipelines, image gen, deployment glue still in place
AI makes each step faster but the stack is preserved
Useful, but not the deepest shift
Most current “AI features” live here
MenuGen example: photo → multimodal model → rendered menu
Frontend, OCR, image API, UI — most of the app disappears
Old stack was scaffolding for a transform the model now does
“Some apps should stop existing as apps”

slides.callout: Do not only ask “what workflow can AI speed up?” Also ask “what information transformation was impossible before but is now natural?”

slides.title:
Build for the human —
or for the human's agent.
slides.subtitle: Most products are still designed for humans clicking screens. The user is increasingly an agent acting on behalf of a human.

points: GUI flows, dashboards, settings panels
Docs assume manual navigation between pages
Onboarding is a tutorial, not an instruction set
Auth and deploy require visual confirmation

right.header: Surfaces an agent can act on
points: Markdown docs, CLIs, APIs, MCP servers
Structured logs, machine-readable schemas
Copy-pasteable agent instructions
Safe permissioning + auditable actions + headless setup

slides.callout: A sensor turns world state into digital information; an actuator lets an agent change something. The future stack is agents using sensors and actuators on behalf of organizations.

slides.section_title:
What founders and engineers
should internalize.
preview: Vibe coding raises the floor; agentic engineering raises the ceiling
LLMs are ghosts, not animals — jagged, alien tools
Understanding cannot be outsourced, even when thinking can

slides.title:
Two truths about the new
professional skill.
slides.subtitle: Vibe coding democratizes creation. Agentic engineering is the discipline that keeps it shippable.

cards.body: Vibe coding lets almost anyone build software by description. Agentic engineering is the professional craft of coordinating fallible agents while preserving correctness, security, taste, and maintainability.
cards.evidence: The MenuGen Stripe-vs-Google email bug: plausible code, bad system design. A human still needs persistent-user-ID judgment.
cards.implication: Hiring should test agentic skill directly: decompose work for agents, write specs, review diffs, harden against adversarial agents.

cards.body: LLMs have no biological drives, embodied survival, or intrinsic motivation. They are statistical simulations of human artifacts — shaped by pretraining, RL, product feedback, and economic incentives.
cards.evidence: Anthropomorphic expectations mislead us. These systems are brilliant in one moment and bizarrely dumb the next — jagged, alien tools.
cards.implication: The right posture is empirical familiarity: learn where they work, where they fail, what they were trained for, and build guardrails accordingly.

slides.footer_note: Source: §7 Vibe vs Agentic · §8 Hiring · §11 Ghosts not Animals

slides.section_title:
Five concrete moves
for founders and leaders.
preview: Make products agent-native end to end
Preserve human understanding as the scarce asset

slides.title: Five moves for founders and engineering leaders.
slides.subtitle: Reorganize the work around agents, sensors, actuators, and verifiable loops.

actions.what: Hunt domains that are valuable, verifiable, and undertrained by frontier labs. Build an environment where agents can try, get reward, and improve.
actions.value: Coding and math are crowded · economically important verticals with latent verifiable structure are the real wedge

actions.what: For each existing app or workflow, ask whether a multimodal model could perform the core transformation directly — dissolving the scaffolding.
actions.value: Some apps should stop existing as apps · don't build faster horses

actions.what: Treat the human's agent as the primary user. Expose CLIs, APIs, MCP servers, markdown docs, structured logs, machine-readable schemas, safe permissions.
actions.value: Auditable actions and headless setup beat click-through tutorials · adoption follows the agent

actions.what: Replace traditional coding puzzles with: build a substantial project with agents, deploy it, secure it, then have adversarial agents try to break it.
actions.value: Tests real ceiling skills · decomposition, specs, review, security, leverage — not memorization

actions.what: Invest in distilled, inspectable artifacts (microGPT-style) and LLM-maintained knowledge bases that turn information into understanding — not just answers.
actions.value: Thinking can be outsourced; understanding cannot · keep judgment in the loop

slides.title:
AI is becoming a new operating
layer for digital work.
slides.subtitle: The scarce thing has shifted — and the work itself is being reorganized.

pillars.body: Software 3.0 made the context window the main lever. December 2025 was a step change: agents stopped being autocomplete and started being a programmable layer for digital work.
Less scarce: code generation, API recall, first drafts, repetitive setup. More scarce: understanding, taste, eval design, security, system boundaries, and knowing when the model is off the rails.
Jagged capability, agent-native infrastructure, verifiable environments, and the gap between humans who orchestrate agents skillfully and those who do not.

slides.closing_line: Define the context, define the tools, define the feedback loop, define the guardrails — let agents work, and preserve human understanding.

slides.title: Thank you.
slides.subtitle: Curated executive briefs · aidigest.club / reads
slides.authors_line: Karpathy, A. (2026). Sequoia Ascent 2026: Software 3.0, Agentic Engineering, and Jagged Intelligence. Fireside chat with Stephanie Zhan.
